destinations.evidence: Phase 2 fee calculation accepts 0x0806 and charges fixed proof-generation fee plus delivery payload fee with zero validator fees.
Async inclusion fees are zero for all async transactions.
destinations.snippet: pub fn async_inclusion_fee(_input_len: usize, _output_len: usize) -> U256 { U256::ZERO }

equivalence_argument: Builder and verifier both call the same Phase 2 fee helper, so ZK fee semantics and zero inclusion-fee behavior are shared.
notes: Mapped in the port fee calculator.
rationale: The commit adds the ZK proof generation fee constant, extends calculate_async_fees_phase2 to branch on 0x0806 and use that fixed fee, and changes async_inclusion_fee to always return U256::ZERO because inclusion is compensated by the original transaction's EIP-1559 fees. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Phase 2 fee calculation must accept both long-running HTTP 0x0805 and ZK two-phase 0x0806; ZK Phase 2 executor fees must be the fixed ZK proof generation fee plus payload fee, while async_inclusion_fee must return zero for all async transactions.
suggested_falsification_tests: Call calculate_async_fees_phase2 for 0x0806 with a nonempty delivery result and assert executor_fee equals ZK_PROOF_GENERATION_FEE_WEI plus http_executor_fee(0, result_len), with zero validator fees. Also assert async_inclusion_fee returns zero for multiple input/output lengths.
summary: ZK Phase 2 pricing and zero inclusion fees are implemented.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Call calculate_async_fees_phase2 for 0x0806 and assert proof fee plus http_executor_fee(0, len), then assert async_inclusion_fee is zero for varied lengths.
title: ZK Phase 2 pricing and inclusion fees
